deep_dive.title: CANARY: Zero-Label Detection of Fine-Tuning Contamination in Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Adversaries can implant latent harmful behavior by poisoning as few as 1% of fine-tuning examples. The contamination is invisible to every output-level defense: harmful behavior lies dormant in the model's hidden-state geometry and does not appear in generated text until contamination exceeds 7.5%. We introduce CANARY (Contamination Auditor via Neural Activation Representation Yield), a zero-label checkpoint auditor that detects this hidden shift directly from two forward passes over an unlabeled prompt set. CANARY projects the hidden-state difference through a Sparse Autoencoder, filtering style noise to isolate meaningful semantic drift. It achieves AUROC = 1.000 at 1% contamination (95% CI = [0.997, 1.000]; Cohen's d = 3.28) across four model architectures and two training paradigms, 7.5x below where any output-level method fires, with zero false positives on benign fine-tuning and full robustness to style-matching and gradient-noise adaptive attacks. The same SAE feature basis drives a complete governance pipeline: SAE-filtered amplification surfaces latent harm at a 5x higher rate than standard generation; score-ranked prompts yield 4.2x red-teaming lift; and suppressing a handful of contamination-specific features at inference time reduces harm from 70% to 10% with no perplexity penalty. CANARY is the first zero-label framework to detect, verify, prioritize, and remediate supply-chain contamination from hidden states alone.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: detects latent harmful behavior from fine-tuning contamination via activation representation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过激活表征检测微调污染导致的潜在有害行为
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过激活表征检测微调污染导致的潜在有害行为
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过激活表征检测微调污染导致的潜在有害行为
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CANARY, a zero-label auditor that detects fine-tuning contamination (poisoned data) from two forward passes by projecting hidden-state differences through a sparse autoencoder, achieving perfect detection at 1% contamination.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对微调数据中毒（仅1%即可植入隐藏有害行为）难以检测的问题，提出CANARY，一种无标签检测器。仅需对无标签提示集进行两次前向传播，通过稀疏自编码器投影隐藏状态差异，滤除风格噪声，隔离有意义的语义漂移。实验表明在1%污染率下AUROC达到1.000，为微调安全提供有效审计工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对微调数据中毒（仅1%即可植入隐藏有害行为）难以检测的问题，提出CANARY，一种无标签检测器。仅需对无标签提示集进行两次前向传播，通过稀疏自编码器投影隐藏状态差异，滤除风格噪声，隔离有意义的语义漂移。实验表明在1%污染率下AUROC达到1.000，为微调安全提供有效审计工具。

quick_skim.title: MESA: Improving MoE Safety Alignment via Decentralized Expertise
quick_skim.abstract: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures scale Large Language Models (LLMs) efficiently, enabling greater capacity with reduced computational cost by dynamically routing inputs to relevant experts, yet introduce a critical vulnerability: Safety Sparsity, where safety capabilities concentrate in few experts, making them susceptible to adversarial bypassing. Meanwhile, conventional alignment methods uniformly adapt all parameters, ignoring their functional differences and inadvertently degrading performances. To address these challenges, we propose MESA (MoE Safety Alignment), a targeted alignment framework for MoE-based LLMs that strategically decentralizes safety responsibility to maximize coverage while minimizing interference with utility. Based on Optimal Transport (OT) theory, MESA operates through two mechanisms: (1) Expert Capacity Reallocation uses a transport cost matrix to distribute safety duties to the most cost-effective experts, and (2) Dynamic Routing Refinement constrains the router to precisely activate these decentralized modules. Experiments show that MESA achieves robust defensive performance against varied harmful benchmarks while preserving helpfulness. Code is available at https://github.com/lorraine021/MESA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MoE safety alignment framework to prevent safety degradation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: MoE安全对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: MoE安全对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: MoE安全对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MESA proposes a decentralized safety alignment framework for MoE-based LLMs to address safety sparsity and minimize interference with utility.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对混合专家模型中的安全稀疏性问题，提出MESA框架，通过分散安全责任来最大化覆盖同时最小化对效用的干扰。MESA基于最优传输理论，在专家间分配安全责任，实验证明能有效提升模型安全性并保持性能。该工作有助于理解安全对齐数据的混合策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对混合专家模型中的安全稀疏性问题，提出MESA框架，通过分散安全责任来最大化覆盖同时最小化对效用的干扰。MESA基于最优传输理论，在专家间分配安全责任，实验证明能有效提升模型安全性并保持性能。该工作有助于理解安全对齐数据的混合策略。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: COMPASS: Cognitive MCTS-Guided Process Alignment for Safe Search Agents
quick_skim.abstract: LLM-powered search agents enable multi-step reasoning and tool use. However, these capabilities introduce retrieval-induced safety degradation, as harmful intents may decompose into seemingly innocuous sub-queries that lead to unsafe outcomes. Existing alignment methods struggle to capture sparse safety signals and fail to supervise diverse violations across multi-step interactions. We propose COMPASS, a Cognitive MCTS-Guided Process Alignment framework designed to achieve robust safety alignment throughout the agent workflow while preserving general utility. COMPASS integrates cognitive tree exploration (CTE) to efficiently synthesize stealthy attack trajectories, and introspective step-wise alignment (ISA) to isolate risky intermediate actions for fine-grained process supervision. Empirical results show that COMPASS achieves a favorable safety-utility trade-off while requiring substantially less training data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: process alignment framework for safe search agents to prevent safety degradation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 安全搜索代理的过程对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 安全搜索代理的过程对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 安全搜索代理的过程对齐框架，防止安全退化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: COMPASS integrates cognitive tree exploration and introspective step-wise alignment to achieve robust safety alignment in LLM search agents while preserving utility.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大语言模型搜索代理中检索导致的安全退化问题，提出COMPASS框架，结合认知树探索合成隐蔽攻击轨迹，并通过内省逐步对齐隔离风险中间动作。实验表明该方法能有效提升多步交互中的安全性，同时保持通用效用。相关工作对安全对齐数据的调度和混合有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大语言模型搜索代理中检索导致的安全退化问题，提出COMPASS框架，结合认知树探索合成隐蔽攻击轨迹，并通过内省逐步对齐隔离风险中间动作。实验表明该方法能有效提升多步交互中的安全性，同时保持通用效用。相关工作对安全对齐数据的调度和混合有借鉴意义。

quick_skim.title: Membrane: A Self-Evolving Contrastive Safety Memory for LLM Agent Defense
quick_skim.abstract: Despite advances in safety alignment, large language models remain vulnerable to continuously evolving jailbreaks. Existing fine-tuned safety classifiers cannot adapt to these evolving attacks, while adaptive memory-based guardrails tend to over-refuse benign queries that resemble stored attacks. We propose Membrane, a self-evolving guardrail built on Contrastive Safety Memory (CSM): each cell pairs the conditions for blocking a harmful query with those for permitting a superficially similar benign request. Without retraining, Membrane evolves CSM by distilling each harmful interaction and its benign counterpart into a contrastive cell indexed by the underlying attack strategy, so that one cell generalizes across topical variants of the same mechanism. At inference, retrieved cells serve as grounding context for precise safety decisions. Across model-level safety on HarmBench and agent-level safety on AgentHarm, Membrane achieves the highest F1 on all six jailbreak attacks. Notably, benign refusal on AgentHarm stays at 7-14%, well below the 28-85% range of prior guards. Memory cells also retain 87-88% F1 under cross-attack transfer and remain stable under memory poisoning.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: self-evolving guardrail against jailbreaks using contrastive safety memory
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用对比安全记忆的自演化防护抵御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用对比安全记忆的自演化防护抵御越狱攻击
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用对比安全记忆的自演化防护抵御越狱攻击
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Membrane proposes a self-evolving guardrail using Contrastive Safety Memory that adapts to new jailbreaks without retraining, effectively blocking harmful queries while reducing over-refusal.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型面对持续演化的越狱攻击时安全防护能力不足的问题，本文提出Membrane，一种基于对比安全记忆的自演化防护机制。它将每个有害交互及其良性对应物编码为对比单元，以攻击策略为索引，使得一个单元能泛化到同种机制的主题变体。无需重新训练，Membrane即可在动态攻击环境中保持高拦截率，同时显著降低对良性查询的过度拒绝。该工作为无需重新训练的自适应安全防护提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型面对持续演化的越狱攻击时安全防护能力不足的问题，本文提出Membrane，一种基于对比安全记忆的自演化防护机制。它将每个有害交互及其良性对应物编码为对比单元，以攻击策略为索引，使得一个单元能泛化到同种机制的主题变体。无需重新训练，Membrane即可在动态攻击环境中保持高拦截率，同时显著降低对良性查询的过度拒绝。该工作为无需重新训练的自适应安全防护提供了新思路。
